content.problemStatement:
You are given two points PPP and QQQ and an opaque sphere in a three-dimensional space. The point PPP is not moving, while QQQ is moving in a straight line with constant velocity. You are also given a direction vector ddd with the following meaning: the position of QQQ at time ttt is Q(t)=Q(0)+d‚ãÖtQ(t) = Q(0) + d \cdot tQ(t)=Q(0)+d‚ãÖt, where Q(0)Q(0)Q(0) is the initial position of QQQ.

It is guaranteed that QQQ is not visible from PPP initially (at time t=0t=0t=0). It is also guaranteed that PPP and QQQ do not touch the sphere at any time.

Find the smallest positive time tvt_vtv‚Äã when QQQ is visible from PPP, i.e. when the line segment connecting points PPP and QQQ does not intersect the sphere.
constraints: the absolute values of coordinates of all points do not exceed 2‚ãÖ1092\cdot10^92‚ãÖ109
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (25 points):** Pz=Qz=dz=cz=0P_z = Q_z = d_z = c_z = 0Pz‚Äã=Qz‚Äã=dz‚Äã=cz‚Äã=0
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains 13 space-separated integers.

The first three integers Px,Py,PzP_x, P_y, P_zPx‚Äã,Py‚Äã,Pz‚Äã denote the coordinates of PPP.
The next three integers Qx,Qy,QzQ_x, Q_y, Q_zQx‚Äã,Qy‚Äã,Qz‚Äã denote the initial coordinates of QQQ.
The next three integers dx,dy,dzd_x, d_y, d_zdx‚Äã,dy‚Äã,dz‚Äã denote the components of the direction vector ddd.
The last four integers cx,cy,cz,rc_x, c_y, c_z, rcx‚Äã,cy‚Äã,cz‚Äã,r denote the coordinates of the centre of the sphere and its radius.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number ‚Äî the time tvt_vtv‚Äã. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed 10‚àí610^{-6}10‚àí6. It is guaranteed that tvt_vtv‚Äã exists and does not exceed 10910^9109.
